metadata.summary: Monitor load balancers with Prometheus exporters, Grafana dashboards, alert rules for backend down/high error rate/high latency/pool exhaustion, and centralized log aggregation.

content.title: Load Balancer Monitoring and Observability
content.summary: LB monitoring requires Prometheus exporters (haproxy-exporter:9101, nginx-exporter:9113), four core alert rules (BackendDown, HighErrorRate >5%, HighLatency p99 >2s, ConnectionPoolExhausted >90%), Grafana dashboards with per-backend breakdown, and centralized log aggregation (ELK/Loki) of LB access logs for audit trail and debugging.

why.p: Load balancers are the single point through which all traffic flows. Monitoring at the LB layer gives the clearest view of user-facing availability: the LB sees every request, every backend failure, and every latency spike before they reach application-layer metrics. Without LB metrics, backend failures are detected only after users complain. Prometheus alert rules with appropriate for: durations (1m for BackendDown, 5m for latency) prevent alert storms from transient blips.

list.item: Setting up observability for a new HAProxy or Nginx production deployment.
Adding Prometheus scraping and alerting to an existing LB that lacks monitoring.
Defining SLIs (error rate, latency p99) and SLOs for a load-balanced service.
Diagnosing intermittent backend health flapping via log correlation.
Cloud managed LBs (AWS ALB/NLB) — use CloudWatch metrics and AWS-native alerting instead; Prometheus exporters are not applicable.
Kubernetes Ingress controller metrics — use the controller's built-in /metrics endpoint with a ServiceMonitor; no separate exporter needed.

example.title: Prometheus scrape configuration for HAProxy and Nginx
example.code:
scrape_configs: - job_name: 'haproxy' static_configs: - targets: ['haproxy-exporter:9101'] relabel_configs: - source_labels: [__address__] target_label: instance replacement: 'haproxy-prod'
- job_name: 'nginx' static_configs: - targets: ['nginx-exporter:9113']
example.note: haproxy_exporter scrapes the HAProxy stats socket (/run/haproxy/admin.sock) or stats page. nginx-prometheus-exporter scrapes the stub_status endpoint. Both are lightweight sidecar processes.

example.title: Prometheus alert rules: BackendDown, HighErrorRate, HighLatency, PoolExhausted
example.code:
groups: - name: load_balancer_alerts rules: - alert: BackendDown expr: haproxy_backend_up == 0 for: 1m labels: severity: critical annotations: summary: "Backend {{ $labels.backend }} is down" description: "Backend has been down for more than 1 minute"
- alert: HighErrorRate expr: | rate(haproxy_backend_http_responses_total{code="5xx"}[5m]) / rate(haproxy_backend_http_responses_total[5m]) > 0.05 for: 5m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "High 5xx error rate on {{ $labels.backend }}"
- alert: HighLatency expr: | histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(haproxy_backend_http_response_time_seconds_bucket[5m]) ) > 2 for: 5m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "High latency on {{ $labels.backend }}"
- alert: ConnectionPoolExhausted expr: haproxy_backend_current_sessions / haproxy_backend_limit_sessions > 0.9 for: 5m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "Connection pool near exhaustion on {{ $labels.backend }}"
example.note: Use for: 1m for BackendDown (fast page), for: 5m for rate-based alerts to avoid false positives on transient spikes.

section.name: key-metrics
section.p: Key metrics to collect and surface per LB type:

list.item: Backend health — haproxy_backend_up, haproxy_server_up per backend server.

section.name: llm-prompts-monitoring
section.prompt:
Generate monitoring configuration for load balancer:
LB type: [HAProxy/Nginx/ALB] Stack: [Prometheus + Grafana / Datadog / CloudWatch]
Requirements: - Key metrics to collect - Alert thresholds - Dashboard panels - Log aggregation
Include: 1. Exporter configuration (if applicable) 2. Prometheus scrape config 3. Alert rules (YAML) 4. Grafana dashboard JSON 5. Log parsing patterns

Define SLIs and SLOs for this load balancer setup:
Service type: [Web app/API/Real-time] Current traffic: [RPS] Business requirements: [DESCRIBE]
Define: 1. Availability SLI/SLO (e.g., 99.9%) 2. Latency SLI/SLO (e.g., p99 < 200ms) 3. Error rate SLI/SLO (e.g., < 0.1%) 4. Throughput SLI/SLO
Provide: - Prometheus queries for each SLI - Error budget calculations - Alert rules for SLO breaches - Runbook templates for common issues

rule.statement: Every production LB deployment MUST have at minimum four alert rules: BackendDown (severity: critical, for: 1m), HighErrorRate greater than 5% (severity: warning, for: 5m), HighLatency p99 greater than 2s (severity: warning, for: 5m), and ConnectionPoolExhausted at greater than 90% (severity: warning, for: 5m).
rule.rationale: Without BackendDown, a failed backend stays in the oncall team's blind spot until users report errors. Without HighErrorRate, a partial outage (one backend returning 500s) goes undetected. Without ConnectionPoolExhausted, the LB silently queues and then drops connections as pool fills.

rule.statement: LB access logs MUST be centralized to ELK or Loki with request tracing headers (X-Request-ID, X-Forwarded-For) forwarded. Log rotation MUST be configured. Raw access logs on local disk are not acceptable as the sole log store in production.
rule.rationale: Local disk logs are lost on instance replacement, unavailable for cross-service correlation, and fill disks under high traffic. Centralized logs provide the audit trail for compliance and the raw data for root cause analysis during incidents.

checklist.check: HighErrorRate alert configured (greater than 1% for warning, 5% for critical).
HighLatency alert configured (p99 greater than threshold, for: 5m).
Certificate expiry alert configured (alert at 30 days before expiry).
Grafana dashboard created with request rate, error rate, latency, connections, backend health.
Access logs enabled and forwarded to central log system (ELK/Loki).
Error logs captured and included in central log system.
Request tracing headers (X-Request-ID, X-Forwarded-For) forwarded to backends.
